content: Single Table Inheritance (discriminator column + nullable type-specific columns on one table) gives best query performance but sacrifices data integrity (many nulls, cannot NOT-NULL subclass columns). Joined/class-table inheritance (base table + per-subtype related tables) gives normalized schema and integrity constraints at the cost of JOINs for polymorphic queries. For a betting platform adding Over/Under and multiple-choice market types alongside existing binary, a discriminator column on the base market table plus separate related tables for type-specific data (e.g. market_options for choices/thresholds) is the standard middle-ground approach.
